item.title: 17 Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
item.description: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

item.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump calls latest peace offer "unacceptable"
item.description: Oil prices spike and the Strait of Hormuz stays paralyzed as Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal and Tehran vows to "fight whenever it is necessary."

item.title: Principal who tackled gunman says he didn't initially know he was shot
item.description: Kirk Moore, the principal at Pauls Valley High School in Oklahoma, exclusively told CBS News how he acted on "just instinct" when tackling a school shooter.

item.title: Trump says he will suspend gas tax
item.description: President Trump made the comments in a phone interview with CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes.

item.title: 13-year-old journalist who has done hundreds of interviews gets major surprise
item.description: At just 13 years old, Sky Ewing had done more than 500 interviews. Her dream to become a reporter was sparked during the COVID-19 pandemic. Ewing recently reached out to CBS News contributor David Begnaud and got the surprise of a lifetime.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 11, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know first.

item.title: 6 bodies found in Union Pacific boxcar in Laredo, Texas, near Mexico
item.description: Six people were found dead in a Union Pacific Railroad boxcar in Laredo, Texas, at the Mexican border, police there said, without offering any details.

item.title: White House ballroom funds hang over GOP push to fund ICE
item.description: The Senate is returning to Washington to resume work on funding immigration agencies with a package that includes $1 billion for the renovation of the White House East Wing.

item.title: Should seniors change their gold investing strategies this May? Experts weigh in
item.description: Certain gold investing strategies could work better for seniors this month. Here's what to consider right now.

item.title: Breaking down Iran's peace proposal response as questions remain over future of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said Sunday that Iran's response to the latest peace proposal is "totally unacceptable." Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told 60 Minutes that the war is "not over" until Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile is destroyed. CBS News' Holly Williams has more.

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect Cole Allen pleads not guilty
item.description: Allen is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump, assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two gun counts.

item.title: Doctor breaks down supplement stacking social media trend and if it actually helps your health
item.description: There's a growing trend on social media called "supplement stacking" where people take multiple supplements everyday in an effort to boost strength, energy and even hair growth. Dr. Rachel Pessah-Pollack explains what to know about the trend.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Flames erupt from Frontier plane after trespasser struck and killed
item.description: New surveillance video shows a trespasser wandering onto a runway at Denver International Airport on Friday. Just minutes later the individual was struck and killed by a Frontier plane taking off. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Record-setting Memorial Day travel expected despite high gas and flight prices
item.description: Memorial Day travel is expected to set another record this year despite surging prices for gas and flights. AAA anticipates 45 million Americans to travel at least 50 miles from home. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Trump calls Iran's peace proposal "totally unacceptable" as gas prices rise
item.description: President Trump on Sunday rejected Iran's latest peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." With no agreement in sight, Trump officials for the first time over the weekend floated the idea of pausing the national gas tax as prices continue to rise.

item.title: Years after sex abuse scandal, a young gymnast says "it happened again"
item.description: Sean Gardner, a gymnastics coach who trained elite young girls, will be in federal court in Mississippi on Monday facing 12 felony counts of sexual exploitation of children.

item.title: Matt Damon and Ben Affleck sued over recent movie inspired by a true story
item.description: Inspired by a true story, Netflix's "The Rip," starring Ben Affleck and Matt Damon, follows two Miami-Dade police officers as they discover more than $20 million of cartel cash during a drug raid and reveals corruption within the department. But now the real-life officers involved in the raid are suing Damon and Affleck through their production company. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Jimmy Lai's son says his father will die in prison if he's not saved soon
item.description: President Trump is set to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping this week in Beijing, where Taiwan is expected to be a key issue. Sebastian Lai, the son of imprisoned pro-democracy media mogul Jimmy Lai, told "CBS News Mornings" that his father will die in prison if he is not saved soon.

item.title: Gymnast says coach sexually abused her despite USA Gymnastics' vow to change: "It happened again"
item.description: USA Gymnastics vowed to make changes after more than 150 women said they were sexually abused by former team doctor Larry Nassar. However, years later, gymnast Finley Weldon is accusing her former coach of sexually abusing her while she was training. "They were brave and they spoke out [about Larry Nassar] so this wouldn't happen again. And it happened again," Weldon said in an exclusive interview with CBS News. Jim Axelrod has more.

item.title: American passenger who was on hantavirus-stricken cruise ship tests positive for virus
item.description: One of the 17 American passengers evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has tested positive for the virus. They are now under quarantine in Nebraska. Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: Eye Opener: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship back in U.S.
item.description: Seventeen Americans from the cruise ship stricken with hantavirus have returned to the U.S. Plus, President Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Trump calls Iran's latest peace proposal response "totally unacceptable"
item.description: President Trump said Sunday on social media that he thinks Iran's response to the latest peace proposal is "totally unacceptable." Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told 60 Minutes that regime change is possible in Iran. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: 5/10: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship headed back to the United States. Trump says Iran proposal "totally unacceptable."

item.title: Taiwan to be key issue at Trump's summit with China's President Xi
item.description: Taiwan, one of the world's biggest diplomatic flashpoints, will be top of mind for President Xi when he meets with President Trump.

item.title: Sen. Mark Kelly says Pentagon's $1.5 trillion budget request is "outrageous"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly of Arizona said the Trump administration's $1.5 trillion budget request for defense spending is "outrageous."

item.title: Pedestrian fatally struck by Frontier plane departing Denver for LA
item.description: A pedestrian was hit by a Frontier airplane departing Denver for LA late Friday night, the airport and airline confirmed.

item.title: 5/10: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," amid the fragile ceasefire with Iran, Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly join. Plus Democratic Rep. Ted Lieu discusses whether his party can take control of the House in November and former FDA Commissioner Scott Gottlieb has the latest on the hantavirus outbreak.

item.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation
item.description: More than 100 people from a cruise ship dealing with an outbreak of the rare and deadly hantavirus are set to be disembarked.

item.title: What's the required minimum distribution from a $300,000 retirement account?
item.description: Have $300,000 saved in a retirement account? Here are the required minimum distributions you'll be expected to take.
